_source: Figma color variables — file nGsiItayj12cfi3AKbP1mB, Colors page (node 7006:2717)
_note: Primitive color tokens extracted from Figma. These are raw palette values. Do not use these directly in components or UI — consume via semantic tokens in colors.semantic.json. Structural decisions: (1) orange is mapped to 'brand' because foreground/accent and background/accent/* in the semantic layer both reference orange-xxx primitives; (2) red, yellow, green, and blue are grouped under 'utility' because they underlie the error/warning/success/info semantic roles; (3) teal, cyan, lime, purple, violet, and pink are grouped under 'support' because they are used only for chart and support foreground/background contexts; (4) neutral-opacity values use rgba strings because the underlying scale uses two base colors — neutral-950 for steps 0–500 (dark overlays, for light mode) and neutral-100 for steps 600–950 (light overlays, for dark mode); (5) static white/black are separate anchors and not part of the numbered neutral scale.

static._note: Absolute anchors. Always white or always black regardless of theme. Used by semantic tokens that must never invert.

neutral._note: Gray scale from near-white (100) to near-black (950). Step 750 exists in Figma and is used by semantic tokens — do not remove.

neutral-opacity._note: Opacity-based overlay scale. Steps 0–500 use neutral-950 (#1D1D23) as the base color and are intended for dark overlays on light backgrounds (light mode). Steps 600–950 use neutral-100 (#F1F2F4) as the base color and are intended for light overlays on dark backgrounds (dark mode). Values are represented as rgba strings to correctly encode the opacity.

brand._note: Orange — the primary brand and accent hue. All accent semantic tokens (foreground/accent, background/accent/*, border/accent/*) reference values from this scale. Step 600 (#FF6B26) is the pure brand color.

utility._note: Hues that underlie the four semantic utility roles: error (red), warning (yellow), success (green), info (blue). Each hue has 11 stops matching the shared scale convention.

support._note: Extended palette used for chart series, support foregrounds, and support backgrounds. These hues do not map to functional states — use utility.* for error/warning/success/info. Known issue: cyan-500 and cyan-600 share the same value (#61D2FE) in Figma — this appears to be a duplication in the source file. Both are preserved here to maintain fidelity to Figma.

cyan._note: Steps 500 and 600 share the same value (#61D2FE) in the Figma source. Preserved as-is.
